details: The "Mukhyamantri Swasthya Sahayata Yojana" was launched by the Department of Scheduled Tribe, Scheduled Caste, Minority and Backward Class Welfare, Government of Jharkhand, and aims to provide financial assistance for medical treatment to eligible beneficiaries. Through this scheme, monetary support is provided to alleviate financial burdens caused by illnesses, surgeries, or other medical needs.
benefits: The grant under the scheme will be given in the following manner:
If the beneficiary is an adult, then in this case the grant under the scheme
Any kind of illness or surgery
If the duration of treatment in the hospital/clinic is less than 7 days
If the duration of treatment in the hospital/clinic is more than 7 days
If the patient is treated at home
If the patient is treated in a hospital
If the beneficiary is a minor, then in this situation, the grant under the scheme
Funds are directly transferred to beneficiaries' bank accounts via PFMS (Public Financial Management System).
eligibility: The applicant must be a resident of Jharkhand.
The applicant must belong to the Scheduled Tribe, Scheduled Caste, or Backward Class categories.
The applicant must be suffering from the illness or surgery.
Applicants already availing financial assistance from other government schemes are ineligible.
The applicant must be a holder of one of the following ration cards:

offline: The interested applicant should visit (during office hours) the Block Office, District Welfare Office, or Project Director (ITDA) Office and request a hard copy of the prescribed format of the application form from the staff exclusively entrusted to issue and collect filled-in applications.
In the application form, fill in all the mandatory fields, and attach copies of all the mandatory documents (self-attest, if required).
Submit the duly filled and signed application form along with the documents to the Block Office, District Welfare Office, or Project Director (ITDA) Office.

Request a receipt or acknowledgment from the District Differently Abled Welfare Officer to whom the application has been submitted. Ensure that the receipt contains essential details such as the date and time of submission, and a unique identification number (if applicable).

documents_required: Ration Card Copy (Priority Household, Antyodaya, or Green Card).
Medical Certificate (Issued by a Chief Medical Officer or Civil Surgeon for illness or surgery).
Bank Account Details (First-page photocopy of passbook).

faqs.question: Who can apply?
faqs.answer: SC/ST/OBC residents of Jharkhand with a Priority, Antyodaya, or Green Ration Card.

faqs.question: What treatments are covered?
faqs.answer: General illnesses, surgeries, COVID-19, and cancer treatments.

faqs.question: What documents are needed?
faqs.answer: Ration Card, Caste Certificate, Birth Certificate, Aadhaar, Bank Passbook, and Medical Certificate.

faqs.question: Where to apply?
faqs.answer: Submit applications at Block Offices, District Welfare Offices, or ITDA offices.

faqs.question: How is the amount disbursed?
faqs.answer: Funds are sent directly to the applicant's bank account through PFMS.

faqs.question: Can I apply if I’m using another scheme?
faqs.answer: No, dual benefits for the same treatment are not allowed.

faqs.question: Who approves the application?
faqs.answer: The Deputy Commissioner of the district.

faqs.question: What if false details are submitted?
faqs.answer: Legal action will be taken, and the application will be rejected.

faqs.question: Are non-SC/ST/OBC applicants eligible?
faqs.answer: No, only SC/ST/OBC individuals can apply.
